facts_disclosed: Plays rec hockey Wednesdays at Bajko Rink

turns.content: bro my legs are absolutely destroyed right now I can barely walk down the stairs in my building lol I think I went too hard last night
Ha, what did you do? Sounds like leg day at the gym or something intense. Whatever it was, your muscles are probably screaming at you right now.

nah its hockey I play in a rec league every wednesday night at bajko rink and last night we had this insane game went to overtime and I was on the ice way more than usual. my quads and calves are done bro

Oh man, rec hockey OT will do that to you. When you're playing way more minutes than your body is used to, everything tightens up the next day. Bajko Rink — are you in the Boston area? 

For the soreness, a few things that actually help: foam roll your quads and calves if you have a roller, take some ibuprofen, and try to get some light movement in today even if it hurts. Just walking around will help flush out the lactic acid better than sitting still. And hydrate like crazy. What position do you play?

yeah im in boston southie. I play right wing usually but last night our center got hurt in the second period so I was coverin for him too which is why I was out there so much. we won tho so it was worth it no cap the guys were goin crazy after

Worth every sore muscle when you get the W in OT. Playing wing AND covering center is a ton of skating — center's gotta hustle back on D way more than a winger, so your legs were doing double duty. No wonder you're wrecked today.

Seriously though, foam rolling is gonna be your best friend today. Hit the quads, calves, and don't forget your hip flexors — those take a beating from skating too. If you don't have a foam roller, even a lacrosse ball or a PVC pipe works. Do you have to work today? Being on your feet all day after that is gonna be rough.

yeah bro im at a job site right now thats why its so bad im goin up and down ladders and bendin over runnin wire and every time I bend my knees I wanna die lol. I dont have a foam roller but I might grab one after work

Climbing ladders with wrecked quads is brutal, I feel for you. Definitely grab a foam roller — you can get a decent one at Target or Walmart for like $15-20 and it'll be a game changer for your Wednesday night hockey recovery.

In the meantime, if you can take a couple ibuprofen and stretch your quads during breaks today, that'll help. Just grab your ankle behind you and pull — basic quad stretch but it works. And tonight, if you can do like 10-15 minutes of epsom salt bath, your legs will thank you tomorrow. The combo of foam rolling and epsom salts after hockey nights will make Thursdays way more bearable.

thats smart I never thought about the epsom salt thing my girl would probably have some of that around. the foam roller tho for sure im gettin one tonight cuz this is gonna happen every week the league goes all the way through march

Yeah if you're playing every Wednesday through March, you need a recovery routine or you're gonna feel like this every Thursday. Here's what I'd do after every game:

1. Stretch for 5-10 minutes right after you get off the ice (don't skip this)
2. Epsom salt bath when you get home
3. Foam roll before bed — quads, calves, hip flexors, hamstrings
4. Drink a ton of water before you go to sleep

Do that consistently and Thursdays at work will feel completely different. The first week or two of foam rolling might hurt more than the game itself, but it gets better fast.

lol yeah I can already tell the foam roller is gonna be painful but if it helps me not feel like an old man every thursday im in. I love playin hockey too much to quit just cuz im sore its the best part of my week honestly the boys on the team are wicked fun

That's the spirit. Having a crew you actually enjoy playing with makes a huge difference — it stops being just exercise and turns into the highlight of your week. That energy probably helps you push through those overtime minutes too. How long have you been playing with this group?

this is my second season with these guys I joined last year cuz my buddy knew a team that needed another skater and its been the best thing ever. we go to the bar after every game too and just hang its a whole thing dude I look forward to it all week

No reason to quit at all — rec hockey with a good crew is the best. Just gotta take care of yourself so you can keep doing it. You're what, in your 20s? Your body should bounce back quick once you get a routine going.

And honestly, playing more minutes like you did last night will get easier as the season goes on. Your cardio and legs will adapt. By February you'll be able to cover center without feeling it the next day.

yeah im 24 I shouldnt be this sore lol but I also sit in a work van for half the day and then go hard on the ice so my body is confused. alright bro im gonna go grab some ibuprofen from my truck and try to survive the rest of the day appreciate the tips

Ha, the van-to-ice pipeline is a tough transition on the body for sure. Pop those ibuprofens, stretch when you can, and grab that foam roller tonight. You'll be back out there next Wednesday good as new. Good luck surviving the rest of the workday man!
